channel.title: Daily AI Digest
channel.description: Curated AI/ML signals — zero LLMs, pure algorithmic curation

item.title: Warp-as-History: Generalizable Camera-Controlled Video Generation from One Training Video
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV — Camera-controlled video generation has made substantial progress, enabling generated videos to follow prescribed viewpoint trajectories. However, existing methods usually learn camera-specific conditioning through camera encoders, control branches, or attention and positional-encoding modificatio...

item.title: MemEye: A Visual-Centric Evaluation Framework for Multimodal Agent Memory
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV, cs.CL — Long-term agent memory is increasingly multimodal, yet existing evaluations rarely test whether agents preserve the visual evidence needed for later reasoning. In prior work, many visually grounded questions can be answered using only captions or textual traces, allowing answers to be inferred wi...

item.title: RAVEN: Real-time Autoregressive Video Extrapolation with Consistency-model GRPO
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV — Causal autoregressive video diffusion models support real-time streaming generation by extrapolating future chunks from previously generated content. Distilling such generators from high-fidelity bidirectional teachers yields competitive few-step models, yet a persistent gap between the history d...

item.title: Does Synthetic Layered Design Data Benefit Layered Design Decomposition?
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV — Recent advances in image generation have made it easy to produce high-quality images. However, these outputs are inherently flattened, entangling foreground elements, background, and text within a fixed canvas. As a result, flexible post-generation editing remains challenging, revealing a clear l...

item.title: Self-Distilled Agentic Reinforcement Learning
item.description: 📄 New in cs.LG, cs.AI — Reinforcement learning (RL) has emerged as a central paradigm for post-training LLM agents, yet its trajectory-level reward signal provides only coarse supervision for long-horizon interaction. On-Policy Self-Distillation (OPSD) complements RL by introducing dense token-level guidance from a teac...

item.title: ATLAS: Agentic or Latent Visual Reasoning? One Word is Enough for Both
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV, cs.AI — Visual reasoning, often interleaved with intermediate visual states, has emerged as a promising direction in the field. A straightforward approach is to directly generate images via unified models during reasoning, but this is computationally expensive and architecturally non-trivial. Recent alte...

item.title: VGGT-Edit: Feed-forward Native 3D Scene Editing with Residual Field Prediction
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV, cs.AI — High-quality 3D scene reconstruction has recently advanced toward generalizable feed-forward architectures, enabling the generation of complex environments in a single forward pass. However, despite their strong performance in static scene perception, these models remain limited in responding to ...

item.title: NousResearch / hermes-agent
item.description: ⭐ 1.7k GitHub stars today · ⭐ 151.2k total stars — The agent that grows with you

item.title: AI Agents: The Future of Intelligent Business Automation
item.description: 🆕 New article — <div class="medium-feed-item"><p class="medium-feed-snippet">Artificial Intelligence is changing the way businesses operate, and AI agents are becoming one of the most powerful tools for automation&#x2026;</p><p class="medium-feed-link"><a href="https://medium.com/@vishal.walkweltech/ai-agents-th...

item.title: K-Dense-AI / scientific-agent-skills
item.description: ⭐ 654 GitHub stars today · ⭐ 22.1k total stars — A set of ready to use Agent Skills for research, science, engineering, analysis, finance and writing.

item.title: MervinPraison / PraisonAI
item.description: ⭐ 120 GitHub stars today — PraisonAI 🦞 — Hire a 24/7 AI Workforce. Stop writing boilerplate and start shipping autonomous self-improving agents that research, plan, code, and execute tasks. Deployed in 5 lines of code with built-in memory, RAG, and support for 100+ LLMs.
